recipes.title: Borscht
recipes.description: Ukrainian beetroot soup with beef, beans and cabbage, served with sour cream, dill and pampushky garlic buns.
steps: Simmer the beef short ribs in salted water with bay leaves for 90 minutes, skimming the foam.
Sauté the onion, carrot and bell pepper in oil until soft, then add the beetroot, tomato paste and vinegar; cook for 10 minutes.
Lift the beef from the broth, pull the meat off the bones, and return the meat to the pot.
Add the potatoes and simmer for 10 minutes, then add the cabbage and the beetroot mixture.
Simmer for another 15 minutes, then stir in the cooked white beans and the crushed garlic.
Season generously and let the soup rest off heat for at least 15 minutes.
Serve very hot with a spoon of sour cream, plenty of dill, and pampushky on the side.

recipes.title: Varenyky
recipes.description: Ukrainian filled dumplings of pillowy dough with potato, cheese, cabbage or fruit, served with butter and sour cream.
steps: Mix the flour, water, egg, salt and oil to a smooth elastic dough, knead for 10 minutes, then rest covered for 30 minutes.
Sauté the chopped onion in 50 g butter until golden.
Mash the potatoes with the farmer cheese, half of the onions, salt and pepper.
Roll the dough thin and cut into 8 cm rounds.
Place a tablespoon of filling on each round, fold in half and pinch the edges to seal.
Boil the varenyky in well-salted water for 4 minutes after they float.
Toss with the remaining butter and onions, then serve with sour cream and dill.

recipes.title: Holubtsi
recipes.description: Ukrainian cabbage rolls stuffed with rice and minced meat, simmered in tomato sauce until tender.
steps: Core the cabbage and blanch the whole head in boiling salted water, peeling off softened leaves until you have 16.
Boil the rice for 8 minutes so it is part-cooked, then drain and cool.
Sauté the chopped onions and grated carrot in oil until soft.
Mix the meats with the rice, half the sautéed vegetables, salt and pepper.
Trim the thick rib from each cabbage leaf and place a heaped spoon of filling at the base, folding in the sides and rolling.
Combine the remaining sautéed vegetables with the tomato paste, passata and sour cream and pour into a wide pot.
Nestle the rolls in the sauce, add bay leaves, cover, and simmer for 90 minutes until very tender.
Sprinkle with dill and serve with extra sour cream.

recipes.title: Salo
recipes.description: Cured slab of pork fatback seasoned with garlic and black pepper, traditionally served thinly sliced with rye bread.
steps: Pat the pork fatback dry and slice into two pieces if it is one large slab.
Make a paste from the crushed garlic, black pepper, coriander seeds and a pinch of the salt.
Score the skin lightly and rub the paste into the entire surface.
Bury the salo in the rest of the salt in a non-reactive container, with the bay leaves on top.
Refrigerate for 5 to 7 days, turning every other day, until the fat is firm.
Brush off excess salt, wrap in cheesecloth, and store in the freezer.
Slice paper-thin while still very cold and serve with rye bread.

recipes.title: Kotleta po-kyivsky
recipes.description: Ukrainian breaded chicken cutlet stuffed with herbed garlic butter that bursts when cut, deep-fried until golden.
steps: Mash the softened butter with the garlic, parsley, dill, salt and pepper, shape into 4 batons, and freeze until very firm.
Butterfly each chicken breast and pound between cling film to a 5 mm thickness.
Place a frozen butter baton at one short end, season the chicken, and roll up tightly to fully enclose the butter.
Tuck the ends in and freeze the rolls for 20 minutes so they hold shape.
Dredge each roll in flour, dip in egg, then coat in breadcrumbs, repeating the egg and crumb step for a thick crust.
Heat the oil to 180°C and fry the cutlets for 6 to 7 minutes until deep golden and cooked through.
Drain briefly and serve at once so the butter is still molten inside.

recipes.title: Kasha
recipes.description: Ukrainian buckwheat porridge cooked with mushrooms and onions in butter, served as a hearty side.
steps: Rinse the buckwheat under cold water and drain.
Toast the buckwheat in a dry pot for 2 minutes until aromatic.
Add the water and salt, bring to a boil, then cover and simmer over low heat for 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, sauté the onion, carrot and mushrooms in butter until golden and tender.
Stir the vegetables through the cooked buckwheat, season with pepper, and rest covered for 5 minutes.
Sprinkle with parsley and serve.

recipes.title: Mlyntsi
recipes.description: Ukrainian thin pancakes similar to crêpes, filled with sweet cheese, jam or savory fillings.
steps: Whisk the flour, eggs, milk, sugar, salt and melted butter into a smooth thin batter and rest for 30 minutes.
Heat a small skillet, brush with oil, and ladle in a thin layer of batter, swirling to coat the pan.
Cook for 1 minute, flip and cook another 30 seconds, then transfer to a plate.
Repeat with the remaining batter, stacking the mlyntsi as you go.
Mash the farmer cheese with the powdered sugar and vanilla.
Place a spoon of cheese on each pancake, fold in the sides and roll up into a parcel.
Pan-fry briefly in butter until golden and serve with sour cream.

recipes.title: Deruny
recipes.description: Ukrainian crisp potato pancakes made from grated potatoes, served with sour cream and fried mushrooms.
steps: Coarsely grate the potatoes and the onion together into a colander.
Squeeze the grated mixture firmly to remove as much liquid as possible.
Mix in the egg, flour, salt and pepper to a thick batter.
Heat plenty of oil in a wide skillet over medium-high heat.
Drop heaped spoonfuls of batter into the pan, flatten lightly, and fry for 3 to 4 minutes per side until deep golden and crisp.
Drain on paper and serve hot with sour cream and dill.

recipes.title: Pampushky
recipes.description: Soft Ukrainian garlic buns brushed with garlic and dill oil, traditionally served alongside borscht.
steps: Bloom the yeast and sugar in warm milk, then stir in the flour, salt, egg and oil to a soft dough.
Knead for 10 minutes and let rise covered for 1 hour until doubled.
Knock back the dough and divide into 12 equal balls.
Pack the balls together in a greased round tin so they touch and let rise for 30 minutes.
Brush with egg wash and bake at 200°C for 20 to 25 minutes until deep golden.
Whisk the crushed garlic, dill and sunflower oil with a little salt.
Brush the warm pampushky generously with the garlic oil and serve.

recipes.title: Solyanka
recipes.description: Ukrainian sour soup with assorted smoked meats, pickles, capers and olives, finished with sour cream and lemon.
steps: Sauté the onion in butter, add the tomato paste and cook until darkened.
Add the diced pickles and brine and simmer for 5 minutes.
Pour in the beef stock and bring to a simmer.
Add all of the smoked sausage, ham and cooked pork and simmer for 15 minutes.
Stir in the capers and olives and let rest off heat for 10 minutes.
Serve in bowls with a slice of lemon, a spoon of sour cream and a sprinkle of parsley.

recipes.title: Holodets
recipes.description: Ukrainian meat aspic of slow-cooked pork or chicken set in clear, naturally gelled broth, served cold with horseradish.
steps: Place all of the meats in a large pot, cover with cold water, bring to a boil and skim thoroughly.
Add the whole onion, carrot, peppercorns and bay leaves and simmer very gently for 5 hours, topping up with hot water if needed.
Add the salt and simmer for 30 more minutes.
Lift out the meats, strain the broth through cheesecloth into a clean pot, and return to a boil; reduce slightly if not strongly gelatinous.
Pull the meat from the bones, discard skin and fat, and chop finely.
Stir the crushed garlic into the broth and let infuse for 10 minutes, then strain again.
Distribute the meat into shallow dishes, ladle the broth over to cover, and chill for at least 8 hours until set.
Serve cold with horseradish and parsley.

recipes.title: Syrniki
recipes.description: Pan-fried Ukrainian farmer cheese pancakes, lightly sweet and golden, served with sour cream, jam or honey.
steps: Press the farmer cheese through a sieve so it is smooth and free of lumps.
Mix in the eggs, sugar, vanilla, salt and raisins.
Add the flour and stir gently to a soft pliable dough; do not over-mix.
Shape the dough into small flat patties about 7 cm wide, dusting with flour.
Heat the butter and oil in a skillet and fry the syrniki over medium heat for 3 to 4 minutes per side until deep golden.
Dust with powdered sugar and serve hot with sour cream.
